preamble: A shift from the voiceless lateral fricative [ɬ] to the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ], often involving an intermediate stage or merger within a larger sibilant series.

examples.note: Loss of laterality in the reflex of the Proto-Semitic lateral fricative.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
